spec.title: XP3 helper-required and missing-key semantic fixtures

humanSummary.text: The XP3 helper-result fixtures represent helper_required, missing_key, wrong_key, validation_failed, and unsupported protected executable states, validate against the Rust contract, preserve public fixture safety, and do not overclaim production XP3 decryption or extraction support. One non-blocking documentation drift follow-up was found for the expanded helper-result diagnostic enum.

findings.title: Helper-result diagnostic enum documentation drift
findings.description: The helper-result schema and Rust validator now accept the XP3 wrong_key diagnostic, but the key-discovery documentation still presents the helper-result diagnostic enum without wrong_key.

evidence.detail: Audit compared the helper-result schema enum with docs/kaifuu-key-discovery.md and found wrong_key missing from the documented diagnostic set.

findings.impact: Developers reading the documentation may treat wrong_key as invalid even though the public helper-result contract accepts it.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: docs/kaifuu-key-discovery.md includes wrong_key in the helper-result diagnostic enum documentation.
The documented helper-result diagnostic set is checked against the public helper-result schema or covered by a docs drift regression.

proposedDagNode.title: Helper-result diagnostic enum docs drift guard
proposedDagNode.summary: Synchronize helper-result diagnostic enum documentation with the public helper-result schema after XP3 wrong_key fixture support.
deliverables: Docs drift guard for helper-result diagnostic names
acceptanceCriteria: docs/kaifuu-key-discovery.md documents wrong_key as an accepted helper-result diagnostic where the enum is listed.
A regression check compares the documented helper-result diagnostic names with fixtures/public/kaifuu-helper-results/helper-result.schema.json or otherwise fails on enum drift.
auditFocus: No private key material appears in docs examples
